mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS > 100k) signals robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in months with PAYEMS > 100k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.0% indicates tight labor market conditions that pressure wages and inflation, prompting central bank hawkishness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with UNRATE < 4.0% exceed 50% of triggered instances or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand conditions that depress corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with GDPC1 and HOUST declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% exceed 50% of triggered instances or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weakening supply chain capacity that reduces corporate profitability, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods with INDPRO declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) above 3.0% indicates persistent price pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policies, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 3.0% exceed 50% of triggered instances or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand dynamics that boost corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with GDPC1 and RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.0%) indicates stagflationary pressures that depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with INDPRO < 100 and CPIAUCSL > 3.0% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
